title: Rabdosia rivularis
intro: Rabdosia rivularis, also known as Chan Tui in traditional Chinese medicine, is a type of herbaceous perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Rabdosia rivularis include diterpenoid lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Rabdosia rivularis is often prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures, and is also used in combination with other herbs in formulas to enhance its therapeutic effects.
taxonomy: Rabdosia rivularis belongs to the family Lamiaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as mint or deadnettle family. This family consists of over 7,000 species, many of which are used as ornamentals or for their edible or medicinal properties. Rabdosia rivularis is a plant species native to China, and its classification reflects its close relationship with other members of the Lamiaceae family. Within the Lamiaceae family, Rabdosia rivularis is classified in the genus Rabdosia, which is a group of flowering plants that are often used in traditional Chinese medicine. The exact classification of Rabdosia rivularis may also involve subcategories such as species and subspecies.

parts: Rabdosia rivularis is a plant species used in traditional Chinese medicine, and the medicinal parts of the plant are typically its leaves and roots.
